methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
NuScale Power is a public-stage nuclear energy company founded by José Reyes and Paul G. Lorenzini, having raised over $600M. The valuation exercise draws from a nuclear energy comp universe of 11 total peers: three public companies (trading under OKLO, NNE, and SMR) and eight private entities, including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is inapplicable to this analysis. Of the eight same-sector peers identified, fewer than three qualify as same-stage comparables, and no adjacent-stage or similar-valuation-tier peers exist in sufficient number to support a reliable median benchmark. The nuclear energy sector lacks the depth of directly comparable public or private reference points necessary for triangulation via peer multiples. Reliance on an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the specialized capital intensity and regulatory constraints unique to nuclear development.

This analysis therefore cannot produce a defensible valuation range through conventional comparable-company methods. Practitioners should consider alternative approaches—discounted cash flow modeling tied to project-level economics, option-adjusted frameworks reflecting regulatory and deployment risk, or precedent transaction analysis within nuclear M&A—to establish valuation support. The core uncertainty remains the absence of sufficient narrow-peer data; broader sector analysis may obscure material differences in technology readiness, permitting status, and time-to-revenue among competitors.
